subject: Chemistry
title: Hermes Trismegistus: The Mythical Founder
description: One-minute skill: Hermes Trismegistus: The Mythical Founder.

hook.text: Alchemy's legendary founder never existed. Hermes Trismegistus was a mythical fusion of Greek Hermes and Egyptian Thoth.

buildup.text: Texts attributed to him—the Hermetica—blended philosophy, astrology, and practical chemistry into mystical teachings.

discovery.text: The famous Emerald Tablet, supposedly written by Hermes, contained alchemy's core motto: 'As above, so below.'

twist.text: The Emerald Tablet is probably from 6th–8th century Arabia—not ancient Egypt. But alchemists treated it as divine truth.

climax.text: The word 'hermetic' (meaning sealed) comes from Hermes. Alchemists sealed their flasks 'hermetically' to trap vapors.

punchline.text: A mythical author, a forged tablet, real chemical techniques. Alchemy's ironic origin.

quiz.question: Who was Hermes Trismegistus?
options: A mythical figure blending Greek Hermes and Egyptian Thoth, not a real person
The first known chemist in ancient Rome
An Egyptian pharaoh who practiced alchemy
